title: ulistput
text: ulistput Insert an item into an item list. Explain this code long ulistput (UHACThList, intoption, intindex, UCHAR*Id, UCHAR* item); Copy Parameters hList Handle to the list, as returned by ulistnew option Tpe of item to be inserted; possible values are: UITEM_OPTION_NONE —Inserts an item by index. You must supply a value for index ; the value for Id is ignored. UITEM_OPTION_ID —Inserts an item by ID. You must supply a value for Id ; the value for index is ignored. UITEM_OPTION_ID_CASE —As UITEM_OPTION_ID , except that the Id is handled in a case-sensitive manner. For example, it is possible to insert an item with Id " a " and to insert another item with Id " A ". index Index of the item, if the item is indexed Id ID of the item, if the item is associative item Text of the item Return Values Table 1. Values returned by ulistput Value Meaning 0 Success. -165 The supplied handle hList is not valid. -1112 The value of option is not valid. -1203 The value of id is out of range. -1406 Memory could not be allocated or freed. Use Library: ucall Description The variable option determines if the item is indexed or associative. Note that there is no safeguard if you do not provide the expected parameters. For example, if you use UITEM_OPTION_NONE , but fail to supply a value for Id , your program is likely to crash. It is allowed, although not recommended, to mix indexed and associative items in one list. For example, you can insert an indexed item into a list that contains associative items, and the other way around. ulistput inserts an item into the specified list. This function is very similar to the ProcScript command putitem . Example: ulistnew, ulist2uf, ulistdel, and ulistput The following example creates an associative list, consisting of country codes and names, and passes it to a service, which prints it to the message frame. The operation DOLIST in service TESTSVC is defined as follows: Explain this code operation dolist params string thelist : in; endparams putmess "%%thelist" end Copy The 3GL code that creates the list and passes it to service DOLIST is as follows: Explain this code char *countries[] = { "NL", "Netherlands", "GB", "Great Britain", "D", "Germany", "F", "France", "?", "huh?", "I", "Italy", "E", "Spain", 0 }; #include <h3gl.h> void pass_itemlist () { UHACT hEnv, hIn; /* Environment and instance handle */ UHACT hOp; /* Operation handles for OP1 amd OP2 */ long actStatus, procErr; /* Activation status and procerr */ UHACT hList; /* Item list handle */ int i; /* Loop variable */ ueopen(&hEnv); /* Get environment handle */ uinstnew (hEnv, (UCHAR *)"TESTSVC", 0, 0, UDEFAULT_COMM_MODE, 0, &hIn, &procErr);/* Instantiate service TESTSVC */ uinstopr (hIn, (UCHAR *)"DOLIST", 0, &hOp);/* Instantiate operation DOLIST*/ ulistnew(hEnv, &hList); /* Create an item list */ for ( i=0; countries[i]; i+= 2 ) /* Loop through the data */ { ulistput(hList, UITEM_OPTION_ID, 0, countries[i], countries[i+1] ); /* Add the list item */ } ulistdel(hList, UITEM_OPTION_ID, 0, "?");/* Delete the item with id "?" */ ulist2uf(hOp, 1, hList); /* Copy item list to operation param*/ uopract(hOp, &actStatus, &procErr); /* Activate the operation */ uinstdel (hIn, 0); /* Destroy instance */ ufreeh (&hList); /* Free list handle */ ufreeh (&hOp); /* Free operation handle */ ufreeh (&hIn); /* Free instance handle */ ufreeh (&hEnv); /* Free environment handle */ } Copy The operation DOLIST prints this line to the message frame: Explain this code NL=Netherlands;GB=Great Britain;D=Germany;F=France;I=Italy;E=Spain Copy Related Links ulistnew Create an item list. ustr2uf Copy string data from a program buffer to a basic parameter or entity field.
